reasoning: As of the forecast date (April 25, 2025), there have been no official announcements regarding changes to the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) components since the previous rebalance in November 2024, which added NVIDIA and Sherwin-Williams (replacing Intel and Dow Inc). Historically, changes to the DJIA are relatively infrequent, averaging less than one per year. Given that a major rebalance occurred only five months ago, and that we have only 66 days remaining until the July 1 deadline with no current rumors of imminent removals or major corporate catalysts (such as large mergers or stock splits) involving the 30 listed companies, the probability is significantly lower than the historical monthly base rate. While companies like Boeing and Nike have struggled, the committee typically waits for structural triggers rather than short-term price performance to make changes. Therefore, a surprise announcement in the narrow May-June window is unlikely.
